title: eremophila decipiens subsp. linearifolia
intro: Eremophila decipiens subsp. linearifolia, also known as the Linear-leaved Emu Bush, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It is a shrub that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of the plant include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and creams, and is considered a valuable resource by the local Indigenous communities for its medicinal and cultural significance.
